perception.who_is_this_for_a: Individual used-car buyers who want to enter a VIN and buy one official Carfax report immediately, with a clearly visible parallel path for dealers needing volume access.
perception.who_is_this_for_b: Price-sensitive buyers comparing carfax.com pricing against a discounted reseller, with dealerships also supported but less prominently in the first viewport.

reasoning: Candidate A better satisfies the brief's high-trust, high-immediacy dual-path requirement. In the first desktop viewport it shows the official Carfax offer, exact price, savings cue, VIN purchase form, sample report link, dealer access card with volume pricing, refund/security/authenticity cues, and a visible trust strip with reports delivered, BBB, Stripe, and registration details. On mobile, the user can still see the dealer path, the official report headline, the VIN form, the purchase CTA, trust bullets, and the sample report link quickly. Candidate B is polished and has a strong savings comparison, clear official-report language, and good later-page trust content, but its first viewport is more single-purchase and savings-led; the dealer path is mostly a nav/link above the fold rather than a serious early self-selection path. On mobile, B's first screen spends much of the space on header and price comparison, with the primary VIN action not visible yet. That makes A stronger for the stated need: fast purchase plus credible dealer self-selection before skeptical users bounce.
blocking_visual_issue_b: Mobile first viewport does not expose the primary VIN purchase action, and the dealer path is not materially presented as a serious early option.
